title: Berula imbricata
taxonomy: Berula imbricata is classified under the family Apiaceae, which is a large and diverse group of plants commonly known as the carrot or parsley family. This classification is based on its shared characteristics and morphological features with other plants in the family. The genus Berula is further grouped into the order Apiales, which includes other aquatic plants. Berula imbricata is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa, and its classification reflects its geographical distribution. The specific classification of Berula imbricata is Berula imbricata L. (Linnaeus).
uses: Berula imbricata, also known as the hook pepper or hook pepperwort, is a plant with various traditional medicinal uses.
